commit.message:
refactor: use initialDocument instead of imperative init

Replace useEffect-based initializeDocument() with a plain PlainNode
object passed to useDenicek({ initialDocument }). Cleaner, no side
effects, and the document structure is now declarative.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Feature new drop system (#1254)

* Add NPC drop type and loading logic

Introduce t_NpcItemDrop and add QuantityOfDrops + drop arrays to NPC types. Update LoadNpcInfoIntoCache and InitializeNpcFromInfo to read per-drop fields (ItemIndex, DropChance, Low/HighQuantityBound) from NPC config, ReDim the drops array when present, and Erase it when none are defined. This enables NPCs to hold multiple item drop definitions loaded from the NPC data.

* Update Declares.bas

* Rename NPC drops array and add assert

Rename t_Npc field Drops() to Drop() and update usages in LoadNpcInfoIntoCache accordingly (ReDim .Drop, populate .Drop(i), Erase .Drop). Also add a Debug.Assert NpcNumber <> 1638 before reading drop info. These changes fix inconsistent naming and align the loader with the updated structure, preventing mismatches when resizing or erasing the drops array.

* Refactor NPC drop system; remove global table

Replace the global drop table with per-NPC drop data and update drop logic. Added QuantityOfDrops and Drop() to t_Npc (plus related fields), removed GlobalDropTable and LoadGlobalDropTable, and stopped loading the old table. Refactored NPC death/drop flow: NPC_TIRAR_ITEMS now skips inventory drops when NPC uses new drop entries, NpcDropeo was renamed/refactored to NpcDropObj to support both legacy Quiza lists and the new Drop array, and DropObjQuest was renamed to NpcDropQuestObj with clearer early exits. Updated NPC initialization (LoadNpcInfoIntoCache / InitializeNpcFromInfo) to populate QuantityOfDrops and Drop entries. Also small naming/capitalization and cleanup changes (e.g. spawn message name property). These changes migrate drop behavior to the new per-NPC system and remove the old global drop mechanism.

* Update Modulo_InventANDobj.bas

* Refactor NPC drop parsing; remove drop sound

Removed the t_GlobalDrop type and refactored NPC drop handling.

- Declares.bas: Deleted unused t_GlobalDrop type.
- MODULO_NPCs.bas: Read drop data from a single 'DropN' string and parse fields with ReadField('-', ...), and replaced element-wise copy with a direct .Drop = Info.Drop assignment to simplify initialization.
- Modulo_InventANDobj.bas: Removed SendData calls that played the drop sound when NPCs drop items.

These changes simplify drop data parsing, reduce duplicate assignments, and stop redundant sound sends on drops.

* Update Codigo/MODULO_NPCs.bas

Added Public Const MaxDropCount = 12 and use it to clamp drop counts instead of the hardcoded 255. Changed INI parsing to read the "DropCount" key when loading NPC info. Adjusted ReDim and iteration ranges to use DropCount. Also fixed NpcDropQuest to skip invalid quest entries (use GoTo NextQuest) rather than exiting the sub. Note: INI files need the new DropCount key (legacy QuantityOfDrops is no longer read).

anime: VIP URL resolution + better logging + site diagnostics endpoint

- Add tryResolveEpisodeUrl(): extracts direct stream URL from VIP player wrapper URLs
  (common pattern: https://jx.example.com/?url=https://cdn.example.com/video.m3u8)
- parseAllRoutes() now calls tryResolveEpisodeUrl() on every episode URL
- /api/tv/stream proxy: on HTML response, try extractVideoUrlFromHtml() before 422
  (some players embed the actual stream URL in JS variables or JSON)
- querySite() timeout: 5s -> 10s per site; hard cap: 15s -> 25s
- querySite() now logs every outcome (0 results, no match, no URL, success)
- Add GET /api/anime/test-sites?name=keyword diagnostic endpoint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(colony-subscriber): Envoak schema compat — session auth, status query, field mapping

3 fixes for colony_state compatibility:

1. Auth: Add COLONY_SESSION_JWT env var. Use session JWT in Authorization
   header (anon key alone is blocked by RLS on colony_state view).
   apikey header still receives anon key for routing.

2. Query: colony_state?status=eq.in-progress (not colony_signals?type=work_claim).
   colony_state is deduped by colony_gc, is_stale computed server-side.

3. Field mapping: read task (branch), files[], agent_label as top-level
   columns from colony_state view — no payload parsing for these fields.
   payload.branch / payload.agent kept as backwards-compat fallbacks.

Release sweep updated: status-based model has no work_release type.
Branches absent from the colony in-progress set are swept from local registry.

commit.message:
feat: automated unit handling for recipes

Auto-create standard recipe units in Grocy (g, kg, dl, l, ml, tl, rkl,
rs, kpl) with global conversions on first recipe scrape. Recipe
positions now use the correct quantity unit instead of always using the
product's stock unit (Piece).

AI-powered package size detection analyses product names via Gemini to
create product-specific conversions (e.g. 'Maito 1L' → 1 piece = 1 l).
Stock comparison uses these conversions for accurate green/yellow/red
status. Shopping list calculates purchase amounts in pieces.

Frontend shows amount + unit next to ingredient names (e.g. '8 dl')
with colour-only stock status instead of meaningless piece counts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Closes #38

* fix: add sudo to devcontainer install-tools.sh for /usr/local/bin writes

onCreateCommand runs as the vscode user which lacks write permissions
to /usr/local/bin. Add sudo to the syft, trivy, and golangci-lint
install commands.

go.mod requires go >= 1.26.1 but the devcontainer image was pinned to
Go 1.24, causing build and test failures inside the container.

Go 1.26.1 has known vulnerabilities in crypto/x509, crypto/tls,
archive/tar, and html/template (GO-2026-4864 through GO-2026-4947),
all fixed in Go 1.26.2. govulncheck now passes cleanly.

commit.message:
Contextualise Hebrew word translations using full sentence

translateWordInContext() sends the full passage to Azure Translator
with includeAlignment=true, then uses word-alignment data to extract
just the Hebrew fragment for the tapped word. This gives context-aware
translations (e.g. 'bat' in a baseball sentence → מחבט, not עטלף).

Falls back to standalone word translation if alignment is unavailable.

commit.message:
Fix dynamic Function new.target leak and generator callable dispatch

- Add push_dynamic_function_call_new_target() helper to isolate
  new.target for BUILTIN_CTOR_FUNCTION/BUILTIN_NEW_FUNCTION calls,
  preventing outer constructor new.target from leaking into
  CreateDynamicFunction and corrupting the prototype chain.

- Fix __fn_body__ callable_expr to use function*/async function/
  async function* for generator/async dynamic functions.

- Split eval+call for generators: eval the function expression first,
  then call it separately so the generator's bytecode outlives the
  eval scope (fixes generators returning {done:true} immediately).

- Route all dynamic generator calls in runner.rs through
  vm_call_function_value instead of manual yield parsing, fixing
  return value handling in dynamic generators.

Cluster 32: 581→595 pass (+14), Cluster 29: 701→704 pass (+3)

commit.message:
Highlight next word and use k-window speech matching

- Next word to read pulses with a soft indigo glow + underline
- Speech assessment uses a sliding window of 5 words instead of
  the entire text, improving accuracy for young readers
- Words are matched strictly one at a time in reading order
  (sequential cursor replaces the old word-text lookup)
- Window auto-advances when all words are assessed, then a new
  recognition session starts for the next 5 words
- Word timing offsets are adjusted cumulatively across windows
  so recording playback stays aligned

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(hostexec): resolve relative argv0 against cwd for rule matching

When an agent runs `cd backend && ./gradlew spotlessApply`, the Python
wrapper sends argv0="./gradlew" with cwd="/workspace/backend". The rule
defines argv0="./backend/gradlew" (workspace-root-relative). matchRule
compared these directly and failed to match, causing a fallback error:
"relative argv0 fallback is not supported".

Fix matchRule to accept an optional MatchContext (cwd + workspaceRoot).
When direct relative-path comparison fails, resolve the actual argv0
against cwd to an absolute path, then re-derive the workspace-relative
path for comparison. This is done in the broker (TS) where both cwd and
workspaceRoot are already available, avoiding changes to the Python
wrapper or new environment variables.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
